memoryArtifacts.headline: FOWLER FAMILY HISTORY FOLLOWS:
memoryArtifacts.description:
FOWLER FAMILY HISTORY FOLLOWS:

FOWLER FAMILY

March 20, 2020
February 14, 2018



 
FOWLER FAMILY
GENERATIONS

More before 1400. I stopped at 1400 (Merle Romer.)

1. Sir William Fowler of Foxley 1 …

Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Colwich, Colwick, Colwiche, Colwike, Colwych, Colwyke, Collwich, Collwike, Collwych, Collick, Collyke, Collich, Colicke and many more. First found in Nottinghamshire where they were anciently seated as Lords of the Manor. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: the name represented in many forms and recorded from the mid-17th century in the great migration from Europe. Migrants settled in the eastern seaboard from Newfoundland, to Maine, to Virginia, the Carolinas, and to the islands. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Recorded in the spellings of Collick, Colleck, and originally (in Cornish registers) as Collock, this is a Cornish locational surname of probably Old English pre 7th century origins. The surname predates the church registers of the area. According to our information the surname is first recorded in the London area, see below, and in the parish of Veryan near Tregoney from about 1680. It is almost certain that the unpublished rolls, charters and wills, in the county Reference Library of Cornwall will give earlier examples of the surname. According to the Cornish Surnames Book the translation of the name is 'little hazel', however we must differ from this opinion, as this is illogical. Our research and experience suggests that the meaning is the same as for the surname 'Coll(e)y' for which the translation is correctly given as 'the place of the hazel'. Of the meaning of 'coll' there can be little doubt, the word appearing in the same context in both Welsh and Old English. Early examples of the surname recording include John Collock of Veryan, who was a witness at the christening of his son John on 24 January 1664, and Elizabeth Collick, who married Richard Luton at All Hallows, London Wall, on 18 January 1665. Grace Colleck was christened at Veryan on 21 September 1681, whilst James Collick, the son of William Collick was christened at Helston, Cornwall on 29 January 1692. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Patrycke Collycke, which was dated 27 August 1564, a witness at St Michael Bassishaw, London, during the reign of Queen Elizabeth 1st, known as 'Good Queen Bess', 1558 - 1603. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Colwick於1459年出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾福可利時，她的父親Humphrey Colwick30歲，母親Lady Jane Clark21歲。她和Sir William Fowler至少有8個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1506年在英國倫敦享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 曼瑟爾。

nameMeanings.name: Colwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Colwick (Nottinghamshire), but in the West Midlands perhaps alternatively from Colewick (lost, in Saint Johns in Bedwardine, Worcestershire) or Colewich (Staffordshire), for which see also Colledge . The placenames are formed either with the Old English personal name Cola or with Old English col ‘coal, charcoal’ + Old English wic ‘dairy farm’ (see Wick ). The surname Colwick is no longer found in Britain. Compare Collick .

variants.name: Collick
meanings: nickname from Middle English collok ‘tankard, bowl, or pail’.

habitational name from any of several places, including Colhook Common in Petworth (Sussex), Colwick in Nottinghamshire (see   Colwick  ), and perhaps a lost or unidentified place in Somerset or Devon.

variants.name: Colledge
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): habitational name from Colwich in Staffordshire, named from Old English col ‘(char)coal’ + wīc ‘building’. The name has become associated with the familiar word college.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Colwick was born in 1459, in Foxley, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Humphrey Colwick, was 30 and her mother, Lady Jane Clark, was 21. She had at least 8 sons and 2 daughters with Sir William Fowler. She died in 1506, in London, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in Frampton Mansell, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles avec Sir William Fowler. Elle est décédée en 1506, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterrée à Frampton Mansell, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Colwickが1459年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアフォクスリーで生まれた時，父Humphrey Colwickは30歳，母Lady Jane Clarkは21歳であった。彼女はSir William Fowlerとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1506年にイギリスロンドンで47歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン マンセルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Colwick(이)가 1459년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 폭스리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Humphrey Colwick(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Jane Clark(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 Sir William Fowler(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1506년 영국 런던에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 맨셀에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Colwick родилась в 1459 г. в местности фоксли, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Humphrey Colwick было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Jane Clark, 21. У неё было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери от Sir William Fowler. Она умерла в 1506 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и была похоронена в местности фрамптон манселл, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
